title: reseda alba subsp. alba
intro: Reseda alba subsp. alba is a subspecies of the white reseda plant, a flowering perennial native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its white flowers and slender, branching stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Reseda alba subsp. alba include reducing inflammation, relieving coughs and colds, and promoting skin health. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
